mechanism_narrative: A higher federal funds rate raises the risk-free rate used in equity discounting, reducing the present value of future cash flows and thereby causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window has absolute value less than 0.03, or t-test of forward returns in high-rate vs low-rate periods fails to show significant negative difference at 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate crossing above 4.5% signals labour market deterioration that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 4.5% level or rises more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: When triggered, the proportion of positive 20-day forward returns exceeds 55% over any 2-year window, or average return is not significantly negative at 1% level.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous high consumer price inflation and weak real GDP growth indicate stagflation, which erodes corporate margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year change exceeds 3% and GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth is below 1%
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger is active, the 20-day forward S&P 500 return mean does not differ from zero at 5% significance over rolling 2-year windows, or the signal's Sharpe ratio is below -0.1.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary easing cycles, rising housing starts signal improving construction activity and consumer confidence, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the longer 60-day horizon as the expansion gains traction.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle (FEDFUNDS declining over 6 months)
failure_condition: The Spearman rank correlation between HOUST and future 60-day returns fails to be positive and significant (p < 0.10) during ease regimes, or the signal is negative in such regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects robust manufacturing activity and aggregate supply, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the short 5-day horizon as economic momentum persists.
failure_condition: Rolling 1-year Rank IC between INDPRO level and next 5-day return is below 0.03, or the directional accuracy over the sample is less than 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly declines in nonfarm payrolls signal rapidly weakening labour demand and imminent recession, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as markets price in earnings deterioration.
candidate_trigger: month-over-month change in PAYEMS falls below 100,000 persons
failure_condition: After a trigger event, the 60-day forward S&P 500 return is positive in more than 55% of cases over any 3-year window, or the median return is not negative at a statistically significant level.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales accompanied by a falling unemployment rate indicate strong consumer spending and a tightening labour market, both supportive for corporate profits and leading to positive short-run S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, the 5-day forward return distribution has a mean less than or equal to zero at 5% significance, or the hit rate (positive returns) is below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: In the post-recession recovery regime, accelerating industrial production reflects a healing supply side that boosts investor confidence and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the 60-day horizon as the cycle matures.
regime_hint: post-recession recovery (GDPC1 positive for 2 consecutive quarters after a trough)
failure_condition: In recovery regimes identified by NBER-based recession dating, the correlation between INDPRO growth and next 60-day returns is not positive and significant (p > 0.10), or the signal produces negative mean return.
